title: ble_uart_send_slip dereferences ipv6 without validating it
description: Trigger: any caller invokes ble_uart_send_slip(NULL, len) with len > 0 while a BLE connection is present. The function validates the connection at firmware-independent gateway code lichen/apps/gateway/src/ble_uart.c:282 and validates len at lichen/apps/gateway/src/ble_uart.c:287, but never checks ipv6 before indexing ipv6[i] in the SLIP encoder at lichen/apps/gateway/src/ble_uart.c:297 and lichen/apps/gateway/src/ble_uart.c:305. Impact: a bad local caller can crash the gateway thread with a NULL pointer dereference instead of receiving -EINVAL; because this is the local client interface path, a missed check here can turn an upstream error into device instability. Fix: return -EINVAL when ipv6 is NULL and len > 0 before acquiring the TX mutex.
close_reason: Added NULL ipv6 validation before SLIP encoding; local diff check passed; Zephyr gateway build remains blocked by existing missing firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys path.
